content:
# Session 32 ‚Äî review of iter-032 (prover, mathlib-build)

## Metadata
- **Sorry: 3 ‚Üí 3** (unchanged, EXPECTED). Live: `H_tensor_invertible` (1.1.2), `H_tensor` (1.1.3), `ext_finite_flat` (1.10). All off the closeable frontier; only 1.1.2 on this iter's lane.
- **Axioms: 22 ‚Üí 22 `External.*`** (net unchanged). The 2 comparison anchors `External.sheafifyTensorComparison{,Left}` were **re-typed** object-level Iso ‚Üí `NatIso` of bifunctors (STEP A) ‚Äî same names, no new axiom (object iso recovered as `.app`).
- **Build exit 0** (8475 jobs, 55s). `lean_verify` axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` on the new substrate (no `sorryAx`, no new `External.*` beyond the re-typed pair).
- **Targets attempted:** `H_tensor_invertible` (1.1.2) ‚Äî STEPs A‚ÄìB + cheap-reduction substrate landed; linchpin `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ` (STEP C entry) blocked, precisely named.
- 209 events: 47 edits, 7 goal checks, 39 diagnostics (11 clean), 13 searches, 23 transient errors (all self-corrected). REPL/diagnostic-driven; final state clean + scratch stripped.

## Target: `H_tensor_invertible` (1.1.2) ‚Äî partial

The 1.1.2 anchor-and-assemble route (opened @031) needs the **naturality** of `tensorAssoc`, which is the `NatTrans.naturality` of the comparison anchors. The plan's STEPs were A (re-type anchors ‚Üí NatIso), B (`tensorRight`), C (`tensorRearrangeNatIso`), D (`tensorRightEquivOfInvertible`), E (`homTensorFunctorTwistIso`), F (close via coyoneda). **A and B landed + the entire cheap-reduction substrate; C‚ÄìF gated on the one linchpin `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ`.**

### Landed (7 decls, axiom-clean, lines ~456‚Äì705)
- **STEP A** ‚Äî re-typed `External.sheafifyTensorComparison{,Left}` to `NatIso` of bifunctors; `tensorAssoc` now uses `External.sheafifyTensorComparison.app (P,Q)` (was `NatIso.app ‚Ä¶` ‚Äî that identifier does not exist). Named bifunctor scaffolding: `moduleSheafification`, `sheafifyForget`, `tensorThenSheafify`, `sheafifyFstTensorThenSheafify`, `sheafifySndTensorThenSheafify`.
- **STEP B** ‚Äî `tensorRight L` endofunctor (right-handed mirror of `tensorLeft`).
- **Substrate** ‚Äî `tensorThenSheafify` / `sheafifyFst,SndTensorThenSheafify` **re-built** as explicit hand-built functors (objects unchanged ‚áí anchors + `tensorAssoc` unaffected) so `.map` reduces cheaply; 4 `private` bridge lemmas `tensorLeft_map_eq`, `tensorRight_map_eq`, `sheafifyFst_map_eq`, `sheafifySnd_map_eq`.

### Two real elaboration obstacles SOLVED this iter
1. **`MonoidalCategory.tensor`-based bifunctor `.map` blows up `whnf`.** Fixed by re-building the bifunctors with explicit `obj := ~(tensorObj ‚Ä¶)` / `map f := ~(tensorHom (~f.1).val f.2)`.
2. **`map_comp`/`map_id` of the hand-built bifunctors.** `Functor.map_comp` would not fire on the product-projected `(f.1 ‚â´ g.1)` (`congr 1` left a stuck `MonoidalCategory ?m`). SOLVED with **`erw [Functor.map_comp]`** (plain `rw`/`simp` cannot match the projected `‚â´` instance) + `rw [SheafOfModules.comp_val]`; `map_id` via `erw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]` (must fully-qualify ‚Äî bare `Functor.map_id` resolves to the *monad* `Functor`).
   - Bridge `rfl`s `sheafifyFst/Snd_map_eq` compile ONLY over a **generic** morphism `f`, NOT a pair `(a,b)`: the pair form introduces `(a,b).1` inside `sheafification.map` and `isDefEq` whnfs the huge sheafification plus-construction ‚Üí timeout. Generic-`f` keeps both sides syntactically `~(‚Ä¶)` ‚áí cheap `rfl`.

### The blocker (precise)
`tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ := NatIso.ofComponents (fun Y => tensorAssoc F Y L) ‚Ä¶` ‚Äî its naturality square needs to identify `(tensorRight L).map ((tensorLeft F).map œÜ)` with `(sheafifyFstTensorThenSheafify X).map (tensorHom (ùüô F.val) œÜ.val, ùüô L.val)` (call it `e1`). Forming this **concrete** equation forces `isDefEq` between the two sides' *types* ‚Äî `tensorMod (tensorMod F Y) L  ‚âü  (sheafifyFst‚Ä¶).obj (tensorObj F.val Y.val, L.val)`. Both have head `sheafification.obj` with defeq arguments, but with the *concrete* nested `sheafification.obj`/`tensorObj` the kernel whnfs the sheafification construction ‚Üí 200k heartbeats (verified `set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000` still diverged after minutes). The anchor's naturality squares `External.sheafifyTensorComparison{,Left}.hom.naturality (a,b)` themselves DO typecheck (verified `h1`/`h3`), so the route is sound ‚Äî only the *concrete object equation* is the obstacle.

Attempts (each removed before final build, leaving substrate clean):
- `e1 := rfl` ‚Üí `(deterministic) timeout at isDefEq`.
- `e1 := by rw [tensorRight_map_eq, tensorLeft_map_eq]` ‚Üí timeout (still concrete).
- `e1 := by conv_lhs => rw [‚Ä¶]; conv_rhs => rw [sheafifyFst_map_eq]; dsimp only` ‚Üí `conv_rhs` rewrote to no-goals then dsimp re-triggered the concrete isDefEq ‚Üí timeout.
- `set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in ‚Ä¶ := rfl` ‚Üí still diverging (effectively non-terminating, not merely expensive).

## Targets not attempted (correctly off-lane)
- `H_tensor` (1.1.3) and `ext_finite_flat` (1.10) ‚Äî untouched as instructed; 1.1.3 is downstream of the same naturality layer, 1.10 needs absent relExt.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- None. `sync_leanok` ran (iter 32, sha 79b72da, added 2, removed 0) ‚Äî markers on genuinely sorry-free decls. No laundering observed; no override warranted. No `\lean{}` renames (prover kept all names), no `\mathlibok` candidates (all decls project-local), no `\notready` to strip.

## Notes (LOW)
- `lean_run_code` 1-shot snippets failed early with "No valid Lean project path found" until another LSP tool ran first ‚Äî known tooling order quirk, not a code issue.

## Recommendations
See `recommendations.md`. Headline: 1.1.2 is one named obstacle from STEP C ‚Äî route the `tensorAssocNatIso‚ÇÇ` naturality entirely through the **generic** `*_map_eq` lemmas (never form a concrete object equation). 12 `lean_aux` coverage-debt nodes for the planner to blueprint.
